data.title: Short sellers slash bets against energy stocks in July
data.description:
Short sellers marginally decreased their bets against oil and gas stocks in July, with short interest in the energy sector reaching 2.18% compared to 2.64% in the month-ago period.

The short bets, where traders sell borrowed shares of companies intending to repurchase them later at a lower price, decreased to 438.83M in July from 530.68M in June.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) rose 6% in July, compared to a 6.6% fall in the corresponding period in the previous year.

The S&P 500 Energy sector (NYSEARCA:XLE) rose 2.1% in July, compared to a 2.3% gain in the S&P benchmark index (SP500).

Most shorted S&P 500 energy stocks as of July-end

  • APA Corporation (APA) 11.26% vs 11.01% in the prior month.
  • Texas Pacific Land (TPL) 7.05% vs 6.84%.
  • Diamondback Energy (FANG) 5.13% vs 4.12%
  • Valero Energy (VLO) 4.10% vs 4.46%.
  • Coterra Energy (CTRA) 3.63% vs 3.83%.

Least shorted S&P 500 energy stocks as of July-end

  • Exxon Mobil (XOM) 0.86% vs 0.94% in the prior month.
  • Chevron (CVX) 0.92% vs 3.48%.
  • The Williams Companies (WMB) 1.18% vs 1.17%.
  • ConocoPhillips (COP) 1.39% vs 1.09%.
  • ONEOK (OKE) 1.87% vs 1.76%.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

Oil and Gas Exploration and Production was the most shorted industry within the sector, with 3.41% short interest as of July-end, up from 3.29% as of June-end.

 
 
 

Integrated Oil and Gas was the least shorted industry within the energy sector, with 1.23% short interest as of July-end, compared to 2.00% at the end of June.

data.title: Workhorse and Motiv Electric Trucks enters into merger agreement
data.description:
• Workhorse Group (NASDAQ:WKHS) and Motiv Electric Trucks on Friday said they have entered into a merger agreement to combine in a transaction that will create a North American medium-duty electric truck OEM.
  • WKHS is +15.81% to $2.05.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, following completion of the all-stock transaction, Motiv’s controlling investor will become the majority owner of the combined company, and Workhorse shareholders will maintain a significant equity stake.
  • In connection with the merger agreement, Workhorse has completed a sale leaseback and obtained convertible note financing. The transactions value the combined company at approximately $105 million.
  • Following the closing of the transaction, Scott Griffith, Motiv CEO, is expected to serve as CEO of the combined company and Rick Dauch, Workhorse CEO, is expected to serve as an advisor to the combined company.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Motiv will be merged with a newly created subsidiary of Workhorse in exchange for newly issued shares of Workhorse common stock.
  • Upon completion of the transaction, Motiv’s controlling investor initially will own about 62.5% of the combined company, Workhorse shareholders will own about 26.5% and Workhorse’s existing senior secured lender will have rights to receive common stock that represent about 11%, all of which are subject to certain potential adjustments and additional future dilution.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q4 of 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DeFi Dev purchases $22M SOL, grows treasury to 1.42M SOL
data.description:
• DeFi Development (NASDAQ:DFDV) announced it now holds approximately 1.42M SOL and SOL equivalents on its balance sheet, inclusive of rewards generated through staking and onchain activities.
  • The increase follows the company’s acquisition of 110,000 SOL at an average price of $201.68, representing a total value of about $22 million.
  • DFDV is -6.59% to $18.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NusaTrip prices $15M IPO, set to begin trading on Nasdaq as "NUTR"
data.description:
• NusaTrip (NASDAQ:NUTR) priced its initial public offering at $4/share, offering 3.75M shares for $15M in gross proceeds.
  • The company granted a 45-day option for underwriters to purchase up to 562,500 additional shares to cover over-allotments.
  • Shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on August 15, 2025, under the ticker “NUTR.”
  • The IPO is expected to close on or about August 18, 2025.
  • Proceeds will go to the company, supporting its travel ecosystem focused on Southeast Asia and Asia-Pacific.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Wall Street is mixed as investors await Trump-Putin summit in Alaska
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages are mixed on Friday, as market participants are keeping an eye on the highly anticipated meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) trades higher by 0.2%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is lower by 0.1%. At the same time, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite is down by 0.2%.

From a sector vantage point, five of the S&P 500’s 11 segments are currently trading in positive territory, with Health Care and Communication Services at the top of the leaderboard. In reverse, Info Tech is the weakest performer in the session so far. 

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields were relatively muted as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) inched up 1 basis point to 4.29%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was lower by 1 basis point to 3.71%. 

Later today, attention will turn to Alaska, where Trump and Putin are slated to meet for talks expected to focus on Ukraine. En route to the summit, Trump stated on the possibility of territorial swaps: 

"They'll be discussed, but I've got to let Ukraine make that decision... I'm not here to negotiate for Ukraine. I'm here to get them at a table." 

On the economic calendar, July retail sales rose slightly less than expected and core sales came in line with forecasts. Import prices rose more than expected in July, and the export prices inched up. 

Industrial production edged down in July while manufacturing output remained unchanged.

Additionally, the U.S. consumer sentiment index slid to 58.6 in August from 61.7 in July and business inventories rose, as expected, in the month of June.

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) popped more than 10% after Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway disclosed in a regulatory filing that it purchased more than 5M shares of the insurer last quarter. 

Applied Materials (AMAT), meanwhile, tumbled over 11% after delivering fourth-quarter guidance that came in well below Wall Street’s expectations.

data.title: Vision Marine prices $7M public offering; shares down nearly 32%
data.description:
• Vision Marine Technologies (NASDAQ:VMAR) priced a public offering of 3.5M shares or pre-funded warrants at $2.00 each.
  • Gross proceeds from the offering are expected to total $7M before expenses and discounts.
  • A 45-day over-allotment option allows underwriters to purchase up to 525,000 additional shares or warrants.
  • The offering is expected to close on August 18, 2025.
  • Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, working capital, and potential strategic investments or acquisitions.
  • VMAR shares fall 31.7% on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Klaviyo announces secondary offering of 6.5M shares by existing stockholders
data.description:
• Klaviyo (NYSE:KVYO) priced a public offering of 6.5M Series A common shares by existing stockholders.
  • A 30-day option allows underwriters to purchase up to 975,000 additional shares from the selling stockholders.
  • Klaviyo will not receive any proceeds from this secondary offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on August 21, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Delek added as a new long at Hedgeye
data.description:
Delek US Holdings (NYSE:DK) was added as a new long idea at Hedgeye. 

"The EPA is due to give a 2026-27 RVO mandate by the end of October and there is an expectation it will need to resolve outstanding SRE petitions beforehand," Hedgeye analyst Fernando Valle wrote in a note on Friday. "There are several options, some that would crater the RINs market, but we expect the EPA will look to attenuate impact on future RINs, while granting court-mandated relief to impacted small refiners." 

Hedgeye raised Delek (NYSE:DK) to a best long idea from the short bench on its expectation that a favorable resolution could result in material one-time gains (100%) and improvements in profitability, Valle added in the note. 

Shares of Delek (DK) rose 1.6% on Friday.

data.title: Currenc Group announces CEO transition
data.description:
• Currenc Group (NASDAQ:CURR) announced on Friday that Dr. Ronnie Hui has stepped down as chief executive officer (CEO).
  • Alex Kong, Currenc’s founder and executive chairman, will assume the CEO position.
  • CURR is -2.90% to $1.6604
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: KindlyMD uplists to Nasdaq Global Market
data.description:
• KindlyMD (NASDAQ:NAKA) announced its common stock shares have been approved for listing to The Nasdaq Global Market.
  • Trading on the exchange will commence today under KindlyMD's current ticker symbol, "NAKA."
  • KindlyMD was previously listed on The Nasdaq Capital Market.
